[30;1m10 | [0m[35m- ```THREEx.ArToolkitSource``` : It is the image which is analyzed to do the position tracking.[0m
[30;1m11 | [0m[35m  It can be the webcam, a video or even an image[0m
[30;1m12 | [0m[35m- ```THREEx.ArToolkitContext```: It is the main engine. It will actually find the marker position[0m
[30;1m13 | [0m[35m  in the image source.[0m
[30;1m14 | [0m[35m- ```THREEx.ArMarkerControls```: it controls the position of the marker[0m
[30;1m15 | [0m[35m  It use the classical [three.js controls API](https://github.com/mrdoob/three.js/tree/master/exampl[0m

[30;1m20 | [0m[35m- artookit.js is perfect for experimenting, not really for mainstream[0m
[30;1m21 | [0m[35m- artoolkit.js is doing markerbased positional tracking[0m
[30;1m22 | [0m[35m[0m
[30;1m23 | [0m[35m- This requires to have a marker at a given position, you need to setup your environment[0m
[30;1m24 | [0m[35m- markerless positional tracking doesn't need it, it is way more flexible.[0m
[30;1m25 | [0m[35m- So for mainstream usage, markerless is definitly more confortable for the user.[0m
[30;1m26 | [0m[35m[0m
